rows.description: Pd Mr Ramsay his Expences abt ye Hertford: Wood
rows.notes: Description begins with 'Pd' and the surname looks like 'Ramsay/Ramsey' — read as 'Ramsay'. Amount columns (three narrow right-hand columns) are clear: '1' in the pounds column, '15' in the shillings column, '0' pence. Vertical ruling lines are unambiguous. Minor uncertainty in spelling of name and 'Hertford' abbreviation.

rows.description: Pd allowed Mr Buckley plate Money By ye new Bursary
rows.notes: Text reads 'Pd allowed Mr Buckley plate Money ...' Some letters in the phrase after 'by' are ambiguous (interpreted as 'new Bursary'). Amount columns read clearly as 8 pounds, 0s, 0d. Slight handwriting ambiguity in the mid-description.
Short description 'Pd Smiths Bill' is legible. Amount columns unambiguous: 11£ 7s 0d. All currency values within valid ranges.

rows.description: Pd Cook acct Day & pay Dinner & Bills on
rows.notes: Description is somewhat cramped but reads as a Cook account for 'Day & pay Dinner & Bills on' (several words uncertain). Amount alignment is clear: 22£ 5s 6d. Pence digit (6) is distinct despite a small ink blot nearby.
Reads clearly as 'Landtax for year 1758'. Amount 7£ 4s 0d is well aligned and legible.
Description interpreted as 'Plumber & glazier Bill' (the second word is slightly stylised but readable). Amount shows 19£ 19s 0d; shillings at the top of valid range but clear.
The item appears to be for 'Back Garden & Quadrary' (some letters archaic). Time period '1 yr & 1/2' is readable. Amount 2£ 10s 8d is distinct; pence '8' clear.

rows.description: Pd Mr Fletcher for Books given to Mr Allen ye Public Clarke for his trouble in ye Colle: Library
rows.notes: Long description; several proper names (Fletcher, Allen) and 'Public Clarke' visible though some words abbreviated ('ye Colle:' for college/library). Amount columns read 3£ 17s 0d. Slight uncertainty in exact phrasing due to abbreviations and cramped script.

A horizontal rule and a right-hand boxed figure (no description) indicate a subtotal. The three-column total reads '164 5 5'. There is some doubt whether an earlier faint digit to the left belongs to this line; however alignment with the vertical ruling makes these three figures clear.
Short, somewhat abbreviated description; read as 'Com Price Mich' (letters ambiguous). Amount reads 0£ 1s 0d (the pounds column has a small zero/blank, shillings '1'). Moderate uncertainty on text.

rows.description: To Mr Wilmot for Window tax
rows.notes: Description 'To Mr Wilmot for Window tax' is legible. Amount 6£ 18s 0d is clear and within valid ranges.
Reads 'Pd Doe for Battery Books' (proper name 'Doe' readable). Amount 2£ 8s 0d clearly aligned.
Short entry 'Pd for Wine Octr 5' (October abbreviated). Amount is clearly 0£ 10s 0d.
A ruled subtotal for the small group above with no textual label; figures read as 10£ 3s 0d and align with the three narrow columns. Slight shadow across page but numbers are legible.

This centered/labelled line looks like a larger summary total (Latinized heading; read as 'Summe Solutiorum' / 'Summe Solutions'). Amount reads 668£ 4s 7½d — the halfpenny symbol is visible after the pence. Some uncertainty in exact heading spelling due to stylized script.
Heading looks like 'Recepte annui Intrests' (receipt of annual interest). Amount is clearly 791£ 4s 0d. Slight ambiguity in the Latin/old-English spelling of 'annui/annu' and 'interests'.

Label begins 'Deb:' (debits). The rest is abbreviated/unclear; transcribed as 'anni Procedutry' to reflect the manuscript. Amount reads 89£ 13s 5½d — the halfpenny fraction is visible. Moderate uncertainty on description spelling.

A numeric line with no textual label (apparently an intermediate calculation) showing 458£ 17s 5½d. Numbers are visible but there is some light shadowing and slight pen-over writing, producing moderate uncertainty.

Text reads as Latinized 'Deductis Solutis' (deductions/payments). Amount shown again as 668£ 4s 7½d (matches earlier summary). The heading letters are legible though Latinised — amount and halfpenny fraction clear.

Final remainder/ balance line begins 'Rest:' and has a shorthand phrase (read as 'Rest: solved a Do'). Amount reads 190£ 12s 10d. Some of the preceding shorthand words are heavily abbreviated and lightly written, producing moderate uncertainty in exact wording.

rows.description: Offic Comptors approbatus per Presentors solicit
rows.notes: A Latin/English administrative line above signatures. Many words are abbreviated / stylised; reading is tentative ('Offic Comptors approbatus per Presentors solicit' is an interpretation). No monetary figures present.

Signature/name line. First name looks like 'Geo' or 'Gren' (interpreted here as 'Gren: Webber Rectorum'). The word after the name is the office ('Rectorum') and is reasonably legible but some letters uncertain.
Readable as 'Tho Bray' with the office-like word 'Subrectorum' (sub-rector) following. Handwriting is cursive but name is discernible; moderate uncertainty on the exact office word spelling.
Clear signature line reading 'Isak Upton' (or 'Isack Upton') — letters are distinct. No amounts present.
Clear short signature 'J. Fowell'. Very legible, no amounts.
Signature reads 'Geo. Stinton' (George Stinton). Letters readable; no monetary values.

Last signature is heavily stylised and somewhat unclear. Interpreted as 'A. Mountstever' (several letter shapes ambiguous). No amounts present, hence classified as header with low confidence on exact spelling.
